content.claim: Selective (tracking) schools raise short-term test scores of admitted students while producing deteriorating effects on test scores of students left behind, but long-run labor-market gains from tracking are not sustained into adulthood.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Based on single empirical research strand, not multiply replicated internationally
Finding suggests sorting is zero-sum in short term but long-run persistence unclear
Does not measure career outcomes directly
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
